ad.title: Scrub Nurse / Theatre Nurse — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Scrub Nurse — Your Theatre Expertise Has Real Value

Every procedure you assist is a masterclass in precision. That expertise deserves to be recorded — and paid for.

🇪🇺 Nursing is on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced scrub nurses and theatre nurses to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal role in theatre. We capture what that expertise looks like.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lighter than your loupes) and a wristband during your shifts
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your theatre schedule
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what procedures and tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

Your clinical judgement stays yours. We record the expertise, not the decisions.

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income. Earnings depend on your specialism, seniority, and how often you record.

€55–€85 per recorded hour

€220–€340 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€3,200–€4,500 per month (based on 20 recording days)

Theatre nursing is one of the highest-paid specialisms on the Observer Programme. First Observers in a surgical specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings are possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 5+ years of experience as a scrub nurse, theatre nurse, or ODP in an operative role
  • Currently working in theatre — employed, agency, or freelance
  • Hands-on surgical assist work: instrument management, swab counts, anticipatory passing, sterile field management
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All surgical specialisms welcome: general surgery, orthopaedics, cardiac/vascular, neurosurgery, laparoscopic/robotic-assisted, ENT, urology, plastics.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — lightweight, shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person who understands healthcare settings
  • Research-grade biometric wristband provided for all healthcare Observers

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Theatre nursing expertise — the anticipation, the precision, the spatial awareness — is some of the most valuable professional knowledge in the world.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your surgical specialism?
  2. How many years of theatre experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in theatre?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Welder / Fabricator — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Welder — Your Craft Has Real Value

A perfect TIG bead takes years to learn. That skill has real value — and now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Welders and flame cutters are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced welders and fabricators to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal work — striking arcs, running beads, fitting up — and earn extra on top of your regular pay.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift (compatible with welding helmets)
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income. Earnings depend on your welding specialism, certifications, and how often you record. Specialist welding (TIG, pipe, structural) earns more than general fabrication.

€14–€42 per recorded hour

€56–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a welding specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of welding experience
  • Currently working as a welder or fabricator — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on welding: MIG, TIG, stick, flux-core, pipe, structural, or specialist
  • Relevant certifications (coded welder, AWS, ASME, EN/ISO preferred but not required)
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All welding specialisms welcome: MIG, TIG, stick/electrode, pipe welding, structural fabrication, aluminium, stainless, carbon steel.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with welding helmets)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Welding is one of the most physically demanding, precision-intensive skills in manufacturing. Your hand control, your heat management, your technique — that's expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your welding specialism?
  2. How many years of welding experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a welder?

Your craft is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Welder — Your Craft Has Real Value

A perfect TIG bead takes years to learn. That skill has real value — and now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Welders and flame cutters are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced welders and fabricators to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal work — striking arcs, running beads, fitting up — and earn extra on top of your regular pay.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift (compatible with welding helmets)
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income. Earnings depend on your welding specialism, certifications, and how often you record. Specialist welding (TIG, pipe, structural) earns more than general fabrication.

€14–€42 per recorded hour

€56–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a welding specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of welding experience
  • Currently working as a welder or fabricator — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on welding: MIG, TIG, stick, flux-core, pipe, structural, or specialist
  • Relevant certifications (coded welder, AWS, ASME, EN/ISO preferred but not required)
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All welding specialisms welcome: MIG, TIG, stick/electrode, pipe welding, structural fabrication, aluminium, stainless, carbon steel.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with welding helmets)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Welding is one of the most physically demanding, precision-intensive skills in manufacturing. Your hand control, your heat management, your technique — that's expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your welding specialism?
  2. How many years of welding experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a welder?

Your craft is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Warehouse Picker / Packer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Warehouse Picker — Earn Extra Every Shift

You move faster and smarter than any machine. That efficiency has value — and now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Warehouse and logistics operatives are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays warehouse workers to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You don't change how you work. You do your normal shift — and earn extra on top.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lighter than safety glasses) and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income. Earnings depend on your role and how often you record.

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your role or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of experience in warehouse work (picker, packer, goods handler)
  • Currently working in a warehouse — employed, agency, or temp
  • Hands-on physical work: picking, packing, sorting, loading, unloading
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All warehouse roles welcome: manual pick-and-pack, cold chain/frozen, automated warehouse, returns processing, goods-in/goods-out.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Your speed, your route knowledge, your handling skills — that's expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What warehouse role do you do?
  2. How long have you worked in warehousing?
  3. Are you currently working in a warehouse?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your role or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

All warehouse roles welcome: manual pick-and-pack, cold chain/frozen, automated warehouse, returns processing, goods-in/goods-out.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Your speed, your route knowledge, your handling skills — that's expertise worth recording.

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your role or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of experience in front-of-house / service
  • Currently working — employed or freelance
  • Hands-on service work: table service, order management, drink preparation, guest interaction
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: fine dining server, casual dining server, banqueting server, bartender.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Front-of-house work — the multitasking, the spatial awareness, the service instinct — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of venue do you work in?
  2. How many years of service experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in hospitality?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

Reading a room, timing courses, managing ten tables at once — that's hospitality expertise most guests never notice. Now you can get paid for it.

ad.title: Road Worker / Highway Maintenance — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Road Worker — You Build the Routes Everyone Relies On

Laying asphalt, cutting surfaces, managing live traffic — that's skilled work under real pressure. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced road workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats and hi-vis) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of road or highway work experience
  • Currently working — employed or contract
  • Hands-on work: surfacing, line marking, pothole repair, machine operation
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: asphalt/tarmac layer, line marking operative, road surfacing machine operator, road inspection/survey operative, pothole repair crew.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of road work do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in road construction or maintenance?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Livestock Farmer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Livestock Farmer — Animal Husbandry Expertise Has Real Value

Feeding, lambing, milking, managing herds — your instinct for animals took years to develop. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays livestock farmers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour | €1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of livestock farming experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on animal work: feeding, breeding, milking, health monitoring
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: dairy farmer, beef/sheep farmer, poultry farmer, pig farmer.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person, wristband if needed

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of livestock do you work with?
  2. How many years of farming experience?
  3. Are you currently working?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Building Inspector / Surveyor — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Building Inspector — Your Trained Eye Keeps Standards High

Spotting defects, checking compliance, reading structures — that diagnostic skill took years to develop. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced building inspectors and surveyors to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your inspections
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of inspection or surveying experience
  • Currently working — employed or self-employed
  • Hands-on inspection work: site visits, compliance checks, defect identification
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: local authority building control inspector, private approved inspector, structural surveyor, damp and drainage surveyor.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of inspections or surveys do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as an inspector or surveyor?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Civil Engineering Site Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Groundworker — Every Building Starts With You

Foundations, drainage, road formation — you prepare the ground everything else depends on. That expertise has real value.

The Observer Programme pays experienced groundworkers and civil site workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of experience in groundworks or civil site work
  • Currently working — employed or freelance
  • Hands-on work: excavation, drainage, foundations, concreting, paving
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: groundworker (foundations, drainage), road/highway construction worker, bridge construction worker, tunnelling operative, piling rig operator.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of groundwork do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working on site?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

Social Worker — Your Professional Judgement Has Real Value

Home visits, assessments, safeguarding decisions — your field expertise protects the most vulnerable.

The Observer Programme pays social workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during visits and fieldwork
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no client faces, no voices

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour | €1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of social work experience
  • Currently working
  • Field-based work: home visits, assessments, reviews
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: children's safeguarding, adult social care, mental health, court-based.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person, wristband if needed

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your social work specialism?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working?

ad.title: Social Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Social Worker — Your Professional Judgement Has Real Value

Home visits, assessments, safeguarding decisions — your field expertise protects the most vulnerable.

The Observer Programme pays social workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during visits and fieldwork
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no client faces, no voices

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour | €1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of social work experience
  • Currently working
  • Field-based work: home visits, assessments, reviews
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: children's safeguarding, adult social care, mental health, court-based.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person, wristband if needed

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your social work specialism?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (AME) — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Aircraft Maintenance Engineer — Flight Safety Starts With Your Hands

Inspecting airframes, replacing components, signing off aircraft — the safety of every flight depends on your expertise. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced AMEs to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. Aircraft maintenance is one of the highest-earning specialisms on the programme.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€110–€180 per recorded hour

€330–€540 per recording day (based on ~3 hours)

€4,000–€6,000 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 5+ years of aircraft maintenance experience
  • Currently working with valid licence (EASA Part-66 / FAA A&P or equivalent)
  • Hands-on work: inspection, component replacement, fault diagnosis, release to service
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: line maintenance, base maintenance, avionics, helicopter maintenance, military aircraft.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person, biometric wristband

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of aircraft do you work on?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working as an AME?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Rail Freight Operative — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Rail Freight Operative — Keeping Cargo on Track

Shunting wagons, loading intermodal, managing rail cargo — your expertise keeps freight moving. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays rail freight operatives to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of rail freight experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on work: shunting, loading, terminal operations
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: freight train shunter, rail cargo loading operative, intermodal terminal operative.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your rail freight role?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in rail freight?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

Rail Freight Operative — Keeping Cargo on Track

Shunting wagons, loading intermodal, managing rail cargo — your expertise keeps freight moving. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays rail freight operatives to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of rail freight experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on work: shunting, loading, terminal operations
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: freight train shunter, rail cargo loading operative, intermodal terminal operative.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your rail freight role?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in rail freight?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Cobbler / Shoe Repairer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Cobbler — A Dying Trade Worth Preserving

Re-soling, re-heeling, leather repair — your craft is centuries old and increasingly rare. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays cobblers and shoe repairers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour | €1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of shoe repair/leather work experience
  • Currently working
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: key cutting and engraving, leather goods repair, bespoke/orthopaedic footwear.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person, wristband if needed

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of repair work do you do?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working?

ad.title: Security Guard / Door Supervisor — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Security Guard — Earn Extra on Every Shift

Patrolling, monitoring, managing access — your vigilance keeps people and property safe. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays security professionals to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour | €900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of security experience
  • Currently working
  • Valid SIA badge or equivalent
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: retail loss prevention, event security, door supervisor, CCTV control room, critical infrastructure security.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of security work do you do?
  2. How long have you worked in security?
  3. Are you currently working?

No extra hours. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Machine Maintenance Engineer / Industrial Mechanic — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Machine Maintenance Engineer — You Keep Everything Running

Diagnosing faults, stripping gearboxes, aligning bearings — when the line stops, everyone looks at you. That skill has real value.

🇪🇺 Industrial machinery mechanics are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced maintenance engineers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your maintenance schedule
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience in industrial maintenance
  • Currently working — employed or contract
  • Hands-on work: fault diagnosis, mechanical repair, hydraulics, pneumatics, planned preventive maintenance
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: planned preventive maintenance (PPM) engineer, breakdown/reactive maintenance engineer, hydraulic and pneumatic systems engineer, industrial machinery mechanic.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Maintenance engineering — the diagnostic thinking, the mechanical intuition, the speed under pressure — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of machinery do you maintain?
  2. How many years of maintenance experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in maintenance?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Glazier — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Glazier — Precision Work That Transforms Buildings

Every pane you fit, every seal you set — that’s skilled work most people take for granted. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Glaziers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced glaziers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of glazing experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: cutting, fitting, sealing, curtain walling, structural glazing
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: commercial glazier (curtain walling), domestic glazier (double glazing), structural glass installer, vehicle glazier.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Glazing — the material handling, the measurement precision, the sealing technique — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

ad.title: Barista / Café Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Barista — Your Coffee Craft Has Real Value

Pulling shots, steaming milk, managing a rush — your speed and precision behind the machine have real value.

The Observer Programme pays experienced baristas and café workers to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour | €900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of barista/café experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on work: espresso preparation, milk steaming, food prep, counter service
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: espresso barista, speciality coffee roaster, café counter and food prep.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your café role?
  2. How long have you worked as a barista?
  3. Are you currently working?

No extra hours. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Scaffolder — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Scaffolder — Your Work Holds Everything Up

Every tie you make, every platform you level — that's skill built at height, under pressure. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Scaffolders are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced scaffolders to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal work — we capture what that expertise looks like.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats and harnesses) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income. Scaffolding is a specialist trade and earns higher rates.

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of scaffolding experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: erecting, altering, dismantling scaffold structures
  • Relevant tickets/cards (CISRS, equivalent national certification preferred)
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: independent tied scaffolder, suspended access scaffolder, tube-and-fitting scaffolder, system scaffold erector.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Scaffolding — the spatial planning, the load awareness, the working-at-height judgement — is specialist knowledge worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of scaffolding do you work with?
  2. How many years of scaffolding experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a scaffolder?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Search and Rescue (Mountain / Cave) — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Search and Rescue — Extreme Skill in Extreme Places

Mountain rescue, cave extraction, flood response — your expertise in hostile terrain is some of the rarest skill there is.

The Observer Programme pays SAR operatives to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during operations and training
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€55–€85 per recorded hour | €3,200–€4,500 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • Currently active

All roles welcome: mountain rescue, cave rescue, flood rescue.

ad.title: HVAC Engineer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

HVAC Engineer — Your Climate Control Expertise Has Real Value

Every system you commission, every fault you trace — that's specialist knowledge built over years. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 HVAC installers and mechanics are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced HVAC engineers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income. HVAC is a specialist trade and earns higher rates.

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of HVAC experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: installation, commissioning, fault-finding, refrigerant handling, ductwork
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: domestic heating engineer, commercial air conditioning installer, industrial ventilation engineer, refrigeration engineer, heat pump installer.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. HVAC — the diagnostics, the system thinking, the refrigerant handling — is specialist knowledge worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your HVAC specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in HVAC?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Locksmith / Security Systems Installer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Locksmith — Security Expertise That Opens Doors

Picking, rekeying, installing access control — your hands solve problems most people can't. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced locksmiths and security installers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of locksmith or security systems experience
  • Currently working — employed or self-employed
  • Hands-on work: lock installation, rekeying, access control, CCTV
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: residential locksmith, commercial locksmith, access control installer, CCTV installation engineer.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your locksmith / security specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a locksmith?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Construction Labourer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Construction Labourer — The Backbone of Every Site

Nothing gets built without you. Your speed, your stamina, your site awareness — that's expertise worth recording.

🇪🇺 Construction labourers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays construction site workers to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — and earn extra on top.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lighter than safety glasses, compatible with hard hats) and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your role or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of experience on construction sites
  • Currently working — employed, agency, or temp
  • Hands-on physical work: groundworks, material handling, site preparation, general construction tasks
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All site roles welcome: general site labourer, groundworks labourer, demolition labourer, material handler, hod carrier.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Site work — the physical intelligence, the awareness, the efficiency — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of site work do you do?
  2. How long have you worked on construction sites?
  3. Are you currently working on site?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Construction Labourer — The Backbone of Every Site

Nothing gets built without you. Your speed, your stamina, your site awareness — that's expertise worth recording.

🇪🇺 Construction labourers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays construction site workers to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — and earn extra on top.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lighter than safety glasses, compatible with hard hats) and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your role or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of experience on construction sites
  • Currently working — employed, agency, or temp
  • Hands-on physical work: groundworks, material handling, site preparation, general construction tasks
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All site roles welcome: general site labourer, groundworks labourer, demolition labourer, material handler, hod carrier.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Site work — the physical intelligence, the awareness, the efficiency — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of site work do you do?
  2. How long have you worked on construction sites?
  3. Are you currently working on site?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Gas Engineer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Gas Engineer — Safety Expertise That's Always in Demand

Installing boilers, testing systems, ensuring gas safety — your certified skill keeps homes safe. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays Gas Safe registered engineers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour | €1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of gas engineering experience
  • Currently working with valid Gas Safe registration (or equivalent)
  • Hands-on work: boiler installation, servicing, gas safety checks
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: domestic gas safe engineer, commercial gas engineer, industrial gas process, LPG specialist.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person, wristband if needed

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your gas engineering specialism?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Teaching Assistant / Learning Support — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Teaching Assistant — Your Classroom Support Has Real Value

Supporting learning, managing behaviour, enabling inclusion — your work in the classroom has real value.

The Observer Programme pays teaching assistants and learning support workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your school day
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no student faces, no voices

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour | €900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of TA/learning support experience
  • Currently working
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: general classroom TA, SEND support TA, behaviour support TA, exam invigilator.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of classroom support do you provide?
  2. How long have you worked as a TA?
  3. Are you currently working?

No extra hours. Just your normal school day — with extra income.

ad.title: Sterile Services Technician — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Sterile Services Technician — Invisible Work That Saves Lives

Decontaminating, inspecting, packing surgical instruments — your process keeps every theatre safe. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays sterile services technicians to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of sterile services experience
  • Currently working in a decontamination or sterile services department
  • Hands-on work: instrument decontamination, inspection, pack assembly, endoscope reprocessing
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your role in sterile services?
  2. How long have you worked in decontamination/sterile services?
  3. Are you currently working in this role?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Floor Layer / Tile Setter — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Floor Layer — Your Precision Underfoot Has Real Value

Every tile you set, every edge you scribe — that's craftsmanship people walk over every day without realising. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Floor layers and tile setters are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced floor layers and tilers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a floor layer or tile setter
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: tiling, laying, cutting, screeding, adhesive application, grouting
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: ceramic/porcelain tile setter, natural stone floor layer, vinyl/carpet fitter, resin flooring installer, underfloor preparation specialist.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Floor laying — the layout planning, the cutting precision, the material knowledge — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your flooring / tiling specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a floor layer or tiler?

Your craft is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

Bricklayer — Every Course You Lay Has Real Value

Consistent gauge, clean joints, plumb corners — that's skill you can't learn from a video. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Bricklayers and masons are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced bricklayers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lighter than safety glasses) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a bricklayer or mason
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: bricklaying, blockwork, pointing, repointing, stonework
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: structural bricklayer, repointing and restoration, dry stone waller, block paver, retaining wall builder.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Bricklaying — the rhythm, the consistency, the eye for level — is craft knowledge worth recording.

The Observer Programme pays experienced bricklayers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day

  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: bricklaying, blockwork, pointing, repointing, stonework
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: structural bricklayer, repointing and restoration, dry stone waller, block paver, retaining wall builder.

The Observer Programme pays experienced nurses to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal ward work — we capture what that expertise looks like.

  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

ad.title: Operating Department Practitioner (ODP) — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

ODP — Your Theatre Expertise Has Real Value

Anaesthetics, scrub, recovery — you move through every phase of the patient's surgical journey. That expertise has real value.

The Observer Programme pays experienced Operating Department Practitioners to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal theatre work — we capture what that expertise looks like.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your theatre shifts
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no patient faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of ODP experience in an operative role
  • Currently working in theatre — employed or agency
  • Hands-on work: anaesthetic support, scrub assistance, recovery management
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: anaesthetic ODP, scrub ODP, recovery ODP.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — lightweight, shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person who understands healthcare settings
  • Research-grade biometric wristband provided for all healthcare Observers

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

ad.title: Steelwork / Structural Ironworker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Structural Ironworker — Building the Skeleton of Every Structure

Connecting steel at height, rigging loads, aligning columns — that's precision work in extreme conditions. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced ironworkers and steelwork erectors to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats and harnesses) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income. Structural ironwork is a specialist trade and earns higher rates.

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of steelwork or ironwork experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: erecting, connecting, rigging, welding, bolting structural steel
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: structural steel erector, ornamental ironworker, reinforcing ironworker (rebar), welding fabricator (on-site).

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Structural ironwork — the load awareness, the height confidence, the connection precision — is specialist knowledge worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of steelwork do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in steel erection or ironwork?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Pharmacy Technician — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Pharmacy Technician — Accuracy That Patients Depend On

Dispensing, compounding, checking — your precision keeps patients safe. That expertise has real value.

The Observer Programme pays experienced pharmacy technicians to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no patient data, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of pharmacy technician experience
  • Currently working — employed (hospital, community, or clinic)
  • Hands-on work: dispensing, checking, compounding, stock management
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: hospital dispensary, community pharmacy, aseptic preparation unit, clinical pharmacy support.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person who understands healthcare settings
  • Research-grade biometric wristband provided for all healthcare Observers

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

ad.title: Explosive Ordnance Disposal / Munitions Operative — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

EOD / Munitions Operative — The Highest-Stakes Expertise There Is

Demining, blasting, pyrotechnics handling — your expertise in explosive ordnance is among the rarest and most valuable professional knowledge in the world.

The Observer Programme pays EOD and munitions operatives to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during applicable work phases
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€110–€180 per recorded hour | €4,000–€6,000 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of EOD/munitions experience
  • Currently working (civilian)
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: EOD technician (civilian demining), quarry shotfirer, demolition blaster, pyrotechnics handler.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person, biometric wristband

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your EOD/munitions role?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Forklift Operator — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Forklift Operator — Earn Extra Every Shift

Stacking high, navigating tight aisles, managing loads at speed — that spatial awareness has real value. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced forklift operators to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of forklift experience with valid licence
  • Currently working — employed, agency, or temp
  • Active forklift operation: counterbalance, reach, order picker, or specialist
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All types welcome: counterbalance forklift, reach truck operator, order picker (high-level), articulated forklift, side-loader operator.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of forklift do you operate?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a forklift operator?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: CNC Machinist / Machine Tool Operator — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

CNC Machinist — Precision That Defines Manufacturing

Setting up, dialling in, running to tolerance — that's expertise a machine can't teach itself. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Metal working machine tool operators are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced CNC machinists and machine tool operators to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your production schedule
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a CNC machinist or manual machine tool operator
  • Currently working — employed or self-employed
  • Hands-on work: machine setup, tool changes, programming, running, inspection, fault diagnosis
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: milling machine operator, lathe/turning operator, EDM operative, CNC programmer-operator, manual machine tool operator.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. CNC machining — the setup knowledge, the tolerance awareness, the material feel — is precision expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What machines do you operate?
  2. How many years of machining experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a machinist?

Your precision is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Insulation Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Insulation Worker — Your Work Saves Energy Every Day

Cavity walls, pipe lagging, external cladding — your work is invisible but essential. That expertise has real value.

🇪🇺 Insulation workers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced insulation workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of insulation experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: fitting insulation, lagging, sealing, measuring, cutting
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: cavity wall insulation installer, loft insulation operative, pipe/duct insulation (lagging) installer, external wall insulation (EWI) fitter, acoustic insulation specialist.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Insulation work — the material selection, the fitting technique, the coverage efficiency — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of insulation do you work with?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in insulation?

  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of insulation experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: fitting insulation, lagging, sealing, measuring, cutting
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: cavity wall insulation installer, loft insulation operative, pipe/duct insulation (lagging) installer, external wall insulation (EWI) fitter, acoustic insulation specialist.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Insulation work — the material selection, the fitting technique, the coverage efficiency — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of insulation do you work with?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in insulation?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Insulation Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Insulation Worker — Your Work Saves Energy Every Day

Cavity walls, pipe lagging, external cladding — your work is invisible but essential. That expertise has real value.

🇪🇺 Insulation workers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced insulation workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
