ad.title: Logger / Forestry Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Logger — The Most Dangerous Job in America Deserves to Be Recorded

Felling timber, operating skidders, working in remote terrain — your physical expertise in extreme conditions is rare and valuable.

The Observer Programme pays experienced loggers and forestry workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with logging helmets) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€28–€42 per recorded hour | €2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of logging/forestry experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on work: felling, machine operation, processing
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: felling operative, skidder/forwarder operator, log processor, timber yard, forestry planting crew.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses (compatible with logging helmets), onboarding, dedicated contact person, wristband

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of logging/forestry work do you do?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

Textile Machinist — Your Craft Keeps Fashion Moving

Operating industrial sewing machines, cutting fabric, maintaining quality at speed — your hand-eye coordination has real value.

The Observer Programme pays textile and garment machinists to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour | €900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of textile/garment production experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on work: sewing, cutting, quality finishing
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: industrial sewing machine operator, cutting room operative, quality checking and finishing.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of textile work do you do?
  2. How long have you worked in textiles?
  3. Are you currently working?

No extra hours. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Prosthetics and Orthotics Technician — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Prosthetics Technician — Crafting What the Body Needs

Moulding, fitting, adjusting devices that change lives — that combination of clinical knowledge and manual skill is rare. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced prosthetics and orthotics technicians to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no patient faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of prosthetics/orthotics experience
  • Currently working — employed or self-employed
  • Hands-on work: casting, moulding, fitting, adjustment, fabrication
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: upper limb prosthetics, lower limb prosthetics, spinal orthotics, paediatric orthotics.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person who understands healthcare settings
  • Research-grade biometric wristband provided for all healthcare Observers

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your prosthetics/orthotics specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in this field?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Delivery Driver / Courier — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Delivery Driver — Your Route Knowledge Has Real Value

Loading efficiently, navigating under time pressure, handling parcels safely — your driving expertise has real value.

The Observer Programme pays experienced delivery drivers and couriers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your route
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of delivery/courier experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or gig
  • Active driving and delivery work: loading, route driving, last-mile delivery
  • Valid driving licence for your vehicle class
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: last-mile parcel delivery, grocery/food delivery driver, HGV driver, refrigerated delivery driver, same-day courier.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of deliveries do you make?
  2. How many years of driving/delivery experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a driver?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal route — with extra income.

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of deliveries do you make?
  2. How many years of driving/delivery experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a driver?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal route — with extra income.

ad.title: Dental Nurse / Dental Therapist — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Dental Nurse — Your Chair-Side Expertise Has Real Value

Anticipating the next instrument, managing suction, keeping the field clear — that rhythm took years to develop. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Dental practitioners are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced dental nurses and therapists to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal chair-side work — we capture what that expertise looks like.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lightweight, unobtrusive) and a wristband during your sessions
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your appointment schedule
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what procedures you assisted
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no patient faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a dental nurse or dental therapist
  • Currently working — employed (practice, hospital, community clinic)
  • Chair-side work: instrument passing, suction management, material mixing, patient support
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: chair-side dental nurse (general), oral surgery dental nurse, orthodontic dental nurse, dental therapist, dental hygienist.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — lightweight, shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person who understands healthcare settings
  • Research-grade biometric wristband provided for all healthcare Observers

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Dental nursing — the anticipation, the dexterity, the procedural awareness — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your dental specialism?
  2. How many years of chair-side experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in a dental setting?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Confined Space / Hazmat Operative — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Confined Space Operative — Going Where Others Won't

Tanks, sewers, chemical plants — you work in extreme PPE in environments most people would refuse to enter. That expertise is valuable.

The Observer Programme pays confined space and hazmat operatives to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with breathing apparatus and PPE) and a wristband
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€55–€85 per recorded hour | €3,200–€4,500 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of confined space/hazmat experience
  • Currently working
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: industrial tank cleaner, chemical plant decontamination, sewer/culvert maintenance, asbestos encapsulation.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses (PPE-compatible), onboarding, dedicated contact person, biometric wristband

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of confined space/hazmat work do you do?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

All roles welcome: event setup crew, banqueting kitchen operative, mobile catering, stadium/arena catering.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of events/catering do you work?
  2. How long have you worked in catering?

No extra hours. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Plasterer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Plasterer — A Smooth Finish Takes Years to Master

Float work, skim coats, ornamental details — the difference between rough and flawless is your hand. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced plasterers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of plastering experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: wet plaster, dry lining, rendering, skimming
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: wet plaster (two-coat), dry liner (plasterboard), exterior render, ornamental/heritage plasterer.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Plastering — the float control, the timing, the surface reading — is craft expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your plastering specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a plasterer?

Your craft is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Mental Health Support Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Mental Health Support Worker — Your Presence Makes the Difference

De-escalation, therapeutic engagement, crisis response — your calm expertise in difficult moments has real value.

The Observer Programme pays experienced mental health support workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no patient/client faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of mental health support experience
  • Currently working — employed or agency
  • Hands-on support work: therapeutic engagement, de-escalation, activity facilitation, observation
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All settings welcome: inpatient psychiatric ward, crisis resolution team, forensic mental health, community CMHT support.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person who understands care settings

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What mental health setting do you work in?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in mental health support?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Port and Dockside Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Port Worker — Dockside Expertise That Keeps Trade Moving

Loading vessels, operating heavy equipment, managing cargo — your port skills keep global supply chains running. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced port and dockside workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of port or dockside experience
  • Currently working — employed or contract
  • Hands-on work: loading/unloading, equipment operation, cargo handling
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: stevedore, port equipment operator (reach stacker, container handler), port security, customs inspection operative.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

ad.title: Concrete Placer / Finisher — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Concrete Finisher — The Foundation of Everything

Pouring, levelling, finishing — you work with material that sets once and lasts decades. That timing and technique has real value.

🇪🇺 Concrete placers and finishers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced concrete workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of experience in concrete work
  • Currently working — employed or freelance
  • Hands-on work: pouring, screeding, finishing, cutting, drilling, pumping
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: ready-mix concrete operative, concrete pump operator, floor screeder, concrete cutter/driller, decorative concrete finisher.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Concrete finishing — the timing, the surface control, the material judgement — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of concrete work do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in concrete?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Inventory / Stock Control Manager — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Stock Controller — Your Accuracy Keeps Everything Moving

Counting, reconciling, locating, managing flow — your system knowledge keeps operations running. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays inventory and stock control workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of stock control / inventory experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on work: stock counting, bin location management, goods receiving, reconciliation
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All settings welcome: retail stockroom, warehouse inventory, just-in-time manufacturing.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of inventory work do you do?
  2. How long have you worked in stock control?
  3. Are you currently working?

No extra hours. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Quality Control Inspector — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Quality Inspector — Your Eye for Detail Keeps Standards High

Visual inspection, measurement, NDT — your trained eye catches what others miss. That expertise has real value.

The Observer Programme pays quality control inspectors to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 2+ years of quality inspection experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on inspection work: visual checks, measurement, CMM operation, NDT
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: visual inspection operative, CMM operator, NDT technician, food safety inspector.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of inspection do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as an inspector?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Demolition Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Demolition Worker — Controlled Destruction Takes Real Skill

Reading structures, managing dust, bringing things down safely — that judgement can't be taught in a classroom. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced demolition operatives to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats and respirators) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of demolition experience
  • Currently working — employed or freelance
  • Hands-on work: manual demolition, mechanical demolition, asbestos removal
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: manual demolition operative, explosive demolition specialist, asbestos removal operative.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of demolition work do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in demolition?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Roofer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Roofer — Your Work Protects Everything Below

Working at height, in all conditions, with precision — that's expertise most people never see. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Roofers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced roofers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats and harnesses) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of roofing experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: slating, tiling, flat roofing, metal roofing, flashing, repairs
  • Comfortable working at height
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: slate and tile roofer, flat roof (felt/EPDM) specialist, metal roof installer, green roof installer, roof inspector.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Roofing — the material knowledge, the weatherproofing instinct, the height awareness — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your roofing specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a roofer?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Roofer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Roofer — Your Work Protects Everything Below

Working at height, in all conditions, with precision — that's expertise most people never see. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Roofers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced roofers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats and harnesses) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of roofing experience
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: slating, tiling, flat roofing, metal roofing, flashing, repairs
  • Comfortable working at height
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: slate and tile roofer, flat roof (felt/EPDM) specialist, metal roof installer, green roof installer, roof inspector.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Roofing — the material knowledge, the weatherproofing instinct, the height awareness — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

ad.title: Care Home Worker / Healthcare Assistant — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Care Home Worker — Your Compassion Is Expertise

The way you lift, support, respond, and reassure — that's skilled care built through years of experience. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Healthcare assistants and personal care workers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced care workers to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal care work — we capture what that expertise looks like.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lightweight, unobtrusive) and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your care routine
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no resident faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your role or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of experience in a care setting
  • Currently working — employed or agency
  • Hands-on care work: personal care, mobility support, medication assistance, daily living support
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All care settings welcome: elderly residential care, dementia specialist care, acquired brain injury care, physical disability care, learning disability support.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — lightweight, shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person who understands care settings

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Care work — the physical awareness, the emotional attunement, the safety judgement — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of care setting do you work in?
  2. How many years of care experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in care?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

🇪🇺 Healthcare assistants and personal care workers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced care workers to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal care work — we capture what that expertise looks like.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lightweight, unobtrusive) and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your care routine
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no resident faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€15–€18 per recorded hour

€60–€72 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your role or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of experience in a care setting
  • Currently working — employed or agency
  • Hands-on care work: personal care, mobility support, medication assistance, daily living support
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All care settings welcome: elderly residential care, dementia specialist care, acquired brain injury care, physical disability care, learning disability support.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — lightweight, shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person who understands care settings

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Care work — the physical awareness, the emotional attunement, the safety judgement — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of care setting do you work in?
  2. How many years of care experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working in care?

No extra hours. No second job. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Commercial Diver / Underwater Welder — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Commercial Diver — Subsea Expertise at the Highest Level

Working at depth, welding underwater, inspecting infrastructure in zero visibility — your expertise in one of the most dangerous professions on earth is exceptionally valuable.

The Observer Programme pays commercial divers and underwater welders to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. Commercial diving is one of the highest-earning specialisms on the programme.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during surface prep, deck work, and post-dive tasks
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€110–€180 per recorded hour

€4,000–€6,000 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of commercial diving experience
  • Currently working
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: offshore saturation diver, inland/civil engineering diver, nuclear diving inspector, underwater welding/cutting operative, ROV pilot-technician.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses, onboarding, dedicated contact person, biometric wristband

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of diving do you do?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working as a commercial diver?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Railway Track Worker — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Railway Track Worker — Keeping the Network Moving

Working on live rail, maintaining points, renewing track — that's specialist skill in a high-consequence environment. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced rail track workers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

Railway track work is specialist and earns higher rates.

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of railway track work experience
  • Currently working — employed or contract
  • Hands-on track work: maintenance, renewal, points, crossings, signalling installation
  • Valid PTS or equivalent rail safety certification
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: track maintenance operative, track renewal gang, points and crossing maintainer, overhead line electrification worker, railway signalling installer.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of rail track work do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working on the railway?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Civil Engineer (Site-Based) — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Site Civil Engineer — Your Field Expertise Has Real Value

Surveying, setting out, supervising pours — the work you do on site can't be replicated from a desk. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Civil engineers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays site-based civil engineers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal site work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats) and a wristband while on site
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a site-based civil engineer
  • Currently working on site — employed or contract
  • Hands-on site work: setting out, surveying, supervising construction, inspections, quality checks
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: site civil engineer (road/bridge), structural site engineer, geotechnical site engineer.

Note: This role is for site-based engineers with hands-on field work. Desk-based design roles are not currently part of the programme.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Site engineering — the spatial judgement, the problem-solving, the coordination — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of civil engineering projects do you work on?
  2. How many years of site experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working on site?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Vehicle Mechanic / Auto Technician — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Vehicle Mechanic — Your Diagnostic Skill Has Real Value

Tracking a misfire, replacing a clutch, diagnosing an electrical fault — that mechanical intuition took years to build. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Automotive mechanics and repairers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced vehicle mechanics to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal workshop work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work in the workshop
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your jobs
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a vehicle mechanic or technician
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: diagnostics, servicing, mechanical repair, electrical fault-finding
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: petrol/diesel car mechanic, EV and hybrid technician, HGV/commercial vehicle mechanic, motorcycle mechanic, tyre fitter.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Vehicle mechanics — the diagnostic logic, the tool handling, the spatial awareness under a bonnet — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of vehicles do you work on?
  2. How many years of mechanic experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a mechanic?

The Observer Programme pays experienced vehicle mechanics to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal workshop work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work in the workshop
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your jobs
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a vehicle mechanic or technician
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: diagnostics, servicing, mechanical repair, electrical fault-finding
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: petrol/diesel car mechanic, EV and hybrid technician, HGV/commercial vehicle mechanic, motorcycle mechanic, tyre fitter.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Vehicle mechanics — the diagnostic logic, the tool handling, the spatial awareness under a bonnet — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of vehicles do you work on?
  2. How many years of mechanic experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a mechanic?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.description:

Food Production Worker — Feeding Millions Takes Skill

Production lines, quality standards, temperature control — your speed and consistency keep shelves stocked. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays food production operatives to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour | €900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus.

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of food production experience
  • Currently working
  • Hands-on production work: line operation, packing, quality checking
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: bakery production, meat processing, dairy production, ready meals assembly, drinks bottling/canning.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of food production do you work in?
  2. How long have you worked in food production?
  3. Are you currently working?

No extra hours. Just your normal shift — with extra income.

ad.title: Mechanical / Electrical Engineer (Site-Based) — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

M&E Site Engineer — The Systems Behind Every Building

Commissioning services, coordinating installations, solving problems on the spot — your site expertise can't be learned from drawings. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Electrical and mechanical engineers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays site-based M&E engineers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal site work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats) and a wristband while on site
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a site-based mechanical or electrical engineer
  • Currently working on site — employed or contract
  • Hands-on work: installation supervision, commissioning, snagging, testing, first/second fix coordination
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: building services M&E engineer, manufacturing process engineer (shopfloor), plant/facilities engineer.

Note: This role is for site-based engineers with hands-on field work. Desk-based design roles are not currently part of the programme.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. M&E site engineering — the system thinking, the coordination, the problem-solving — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your M&E specialism?
  2. How many years of site experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working on site?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

🇪🇺 Electrical and mechanical engineers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays site-based M&E engineers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal site work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with hard hats) and a wristband while on site
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a site-based mechanical or electrical engineer
  • Currently working on site — employed or contract
  • Hands-on work: installation supervision, commissioning, snagging, testing, first/second fix coordination
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: building services M&E engineer, manufacturing process engineer (shopfloor), plant/facilities engineer.

Note: This role is for site-based engineers with hands-on field work. Desk-based design roles are not currently part of the programme.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured (compatible with hard hats)
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. M&E site engineering — the system thinking, the coordination, the problem-solving — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your M&E specialism?
  2. How many years of site experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working on site?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Underground Miner / Mining Operative — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Underground Miner — Extreme Expertise, Extreme Value

Drilling, blasting, operating in confined underground environments — your expertise in one of the world's most dangerous professions is exceptionally valuable.

The Observer Programme pays experienced underground miners to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. Mining is one of the highest-earning specialisms on the programme.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (compatible with mining helmets) and a wristband
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised

What you'll earn

€110–€180 per recorded hour

€4,000–€6,000 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of underground mining experience
  • Currently working
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All roles welcome: hard rock miner, longwall coal miner, continuous miner operator, mine electrician, mine ventilation technician, shotcrete operative.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses (compatible with mining helmets), onboarding, dedicated contact person, biometric wristband

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of mining do you do?
  2. How many years of experience?
  3. Are you currently working underground?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Prison Officer — Your Calm Under Pressure Has Real Value

Managing wings, de-escalating incidents, maintaining order — your situational awareness has real value.

The Observer Programme pays prison and custody officers to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up

ad.title: Crane Operator — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Crane Operator — Precision at Scale

Moving tonnes with millimetre accuracy, reading wind, managing blind lifts — that spatial judgement is rare. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced crane operators to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal lifts — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband during your shift
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your lifts
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

Crane operation is a high-scarcity specialism and earns premium rates.

€55–€85 per recorded hour

€220–€340 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€3,200–€4,500 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in your specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of crane operating experience
  • Currently working — employed or freelance
  • Active crane operation: lifting, slewing, load management
  • Valid certifications/tickets for your crane type
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: tower crane operator, mobile crane operator, overhead gantry crane operator, telehandler operator.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Crane operation — the spatial awareness, the load judgement, the environmental reading — is some of the most valuable specialist knowledge on any site.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of crane do you operate?
  2. How many years of crane operating experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a crane operator?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Painter and Decorator — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Painter & Decorator — The Finish That Makes the Difference

Clean lines, consistent coverage, the right technique for every surface — that's skill people notice but can't explain. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Painters and building finishers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced painters and decorators to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a painter and/or decorator
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: preparation, painting, wallpapering, spraying, specialist finishes
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: interior domestic painter, commercial painter (large-scale), industrial painter (protective coatings), heritage restoration painter.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Painting and decorating — the surface preparation, the brush control, the efficiency of movement — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your painting / decorating specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a painter or decorator?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Painter and Decorator — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Painter & Decorator — The Finish That Makes the Difference

Clean lines, consistent coverage, the right technique for every surface — that's skill people notice but can't explain. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Painters and building finishers are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced painters and decorators to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a painter and/or decorator
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: preparation, painting, wallpapering, spraying, specialist finishes
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: interior domestic painter, commercial painter (large-scale), industrial painter (protective coatings), heritage restoration painter.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Painting and decorating — the surface preparation, the brush control, the efficiency of movement — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your painting / decorating specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a painter or decorator?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Retail / Supermarket Operative — Observer Programme
ad.description:

Retail Worker — Earn Extra Every Shift

Stocking shelves, running counters, managing the floor — your speed and customer awareness have real value.

The Observer Programme pays retail and supermarket workers to record their shifts using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no customer faces, no voices

What you'll earn

€15–€18 per recorded hour | €900–€1,100 per month (based on 20 recording days)

What we're looking for

  • 1+ years of retail experience
  • Currently working

All roles welcome: shelf replenishment, self-checkout host, fresh counter operative, click-and-collect picker.

ad.title: Lift / Elevator Engineer — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Lift Engineer — The Expertise Behind Every Floor

Installing, maintaining, and troubleshooting lifts in confined shafts — that's specialist mechanical skill. Now you can get paid for it.

The Observer Programme pays experienced lift engineers to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did
  • Get paid per recorded minute
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of lift engineering experience
  • Currently working — employed or contract
  • Hands-on work: installation, maintenance, fault-finding, modernisation
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: new installation engineer, maintenance and service engineer, escalator engineer, platform lift engineer.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What type of lift work do you do?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a lift engineer?

Your expertise is valuable. Find out what it's worth.

ad.title: Physiotherapist — Observer Programme

Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Physiotherapist — Your Hands-On Expertise Has Real Value

The way you assess, mobilise, and guide recovery — that clinical skill can't be learned from a textbook. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Physiotherapists are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced physiotherapists to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You continue your normal patient work — we capture what that expertise looks like.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lightweight, unobtrusive) and a wristband during your sessions
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your caseload
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what treatments and techniques you performed
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no patient faces, no voices, no identifying details

Your clinical decisions stay yours. We record the physical expertise, not the patient relationship.

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income. Physiotherapy is a specialist profession and earns higher rates.

€28–€42 per recorded hour

€112–€168 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€2,000–€2,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of clinical physiotherapy experience
  • Currently practising — employed (NHS/hospital, private clinic) or self-employed
  • Hands-on treatment work: manual therapy, mobilisation, exercise prescription, respiratory physio
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: musculoskeletal (outpatient), neurological rehab, respiratory/ICU physio, sports physio, paediatric physio.

  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)

ad.title: Site Carpenter / Joiner — Observer Programme
ad.description:
Current: v2.0 · Last edited: 2026-03-24

Carpenter — Your Craft Shapes Everything Around Us

Every frame you raise, every joint you cut — that precision took years to develop. Now you can get paid for it.

🇪🇺 Carpenters and joiners are on the European Commission's official list of critical shortage professions. Your skills are in high demand. Record them. Get paid.

The Observer Programme pays experienced carpenters to record their working day using lightweight smart glasses and a wristband. You do your normal work — we capture the expertise.

How it works

  • Wear smart glasses (lighter than safety glasses) and a wristband while you work
  • Record sessions from 30 minutes up — fits around your day
  • Spend a few minutes after each session confirming what tasks you did in the app
  • Get paid per recorded minute. The more you record, the more you earn
  • All recordings are anonymised — no faces, no voices, no identifying details

What you'll earn

You're paid per minute of accepted footage — on top of your regular income.

€20–€28 per recorded hour

€80–€112 per recording day (based on ~4 hours)

€1,200–€1,800 per month (based on 20 recording days)

First Observers in a specialism or region earn a launch bonus. Extra earnings possible through referral bonuses and specialist recording tasks.

What we're looking for

  • 3+ years of experience as a carpenter or joiner
  • Currently working — employed, self-employed, or freelance
  • Hands-on work: framing, joinery, fitting, formwork, measuring and cutting
  • Smartphone (iOS or Android)

All specialisms welcome: first-fix carpenter (structural framing), second-fix carpenter (doors, skirting, joinery), shopfitter, formwork carpenter, roofing carpenter.

We provide everything

  • Smart glasses — shipped to you, pre-configured
  • Full onboarding and support (~30 minutes)
  • Dedicated contact person
  • Biometric wristband if you don't own a compatible fitness tracker

About the Observer Programme

The Observer Programme by Aisthis works with professionals across 100+ trades to document how skilled work actually happens. Carpentry — the spatial judgement, the material knowledge, the hand precision — is expertise worth recording.

Applications are reviewed with the help of AI. We'll be in touch if there's a match.

Quick apply — 3 questions

  1. What's your carpentry specialism?
  2. How many years of experience do you have?
  3. Are you currently working as a carpenter?

Your craft is valuable. Find out what it's worth.
